Intuitively, speaking of a a su¢ cient statistic means that this statistic contains all the information about the parameter(s) of the probability distribution. We consider a parametric statistical model ( X ; P  ) . The goal is to obtain the maximum possible knowledge about the parameter  from the observation ( X 1 ; X 2 ;    ; X n ) : It is therefore useful to reduce the data by summarizing it through a statistic T of dimension much smaller than n . It is natural to expect that the summary T of the observations contains less information about  than the complete set of initial data. However, there exist statistics that summarize the observations while preserving all the information about  ; these are called su¢ cient statistics. The problem of statistical estimation is as follows: - We seek to determine the values of certain characteristics of a random variable based on observations made from a sample. - Point estimation is performed using an estimator, which is a sample random variable. - The estimate is the value taken by the random variable in the observed sample. Remark 4 When point estimators are frequently used, we want them to possess certain properties.
